---
title: "Figure1"
output: html_document
date: "2025-11-27"
---



```{r setup, include=FALSE}
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(data.table)
library(patchwork)
library(ggvenn)
library(randomForest)
library(forcats)
library(RColorBrewer)

```


## Model Performance and feature importance

model performance
```{r }

library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(tibble)
library(stringr)


#model testing
#HG002
cm_hg002=readRDS("/mnt/beegfs/labs/DiGenomaLab/rfhap/processing/rfhap/results/HG002/HG002_dataset/rf/train/HG002_standar_ont_sup_PAW70337.fastq_fastkm_matrix_confusion_matrix.rds")
#HG01109
cm_hg01109=readRDS("/mnt/beegfs/labs/DiGenomaLab/rfhap/processing/rfhap/new_datasets/HG01109/results/rf/train/HG01109_1_R941_Guppy_6.1.2.fq_fastkm_matrix_confusion_matrix.rds")
#HG005
cm_hg005=readRDS("/mnt/beegfs/labs/DiGenomaLab/rfhap/processing/rfhap/hg005_v2/results/rf/train/HG005_standar_ont_sup_PAW88001.fastq_fastkm_matrix_confusion_matrix.rds")
#HG01243
cm_hg01243=readRDS("/mnt/beegfs/labs/DiGenomaLab/rfhap/processing/rfhap/new_datasets/HG01243/results/rf/train/HG01243_1_R941_Guppy_6.1.2.fq_fastkm_matrix_confusion_matrix.rds")


#---------------------------
# 1) Collect metrics into one tidy table
#---------------------------
extract_byClass <- function(cm, dataset, chemistry) {
  as.data.frame(cm$byClass) |>
    rownames_to_column("Class") |>
    mutate(
      Class = str_remove(Class, "^Class:\\s*"),
      Dataset = dataset,
      Chemistry = chemistry
    )
}

metrics_df <- bind_rows(
  extract_byClass(cm_hg002,   "HG002",   "R10.4"),
  extract_byClass(cm_hg005,   "HG005",   "R10.4"),
  extract_byClass(cm_hg01243, "HG01243", "R9.4.1"),
  extract_byClass(cm_hg01109, "HG01109", "R9.4.1")
) |>
  # keep the metrics you likely want in a supplement
  select(Dataset, Chemistry, Class,
         Sensitivity, Specificity,
         Precision, Recall, F1,
         `Pos Pred Value`, `Neg Pred Value`,
         `Balanced Accuracy`,
         Prevalence, `Detection Rate`, `Detection Prevalence`) |>
  mutate(
    Dataset = factor(Dataset, levels = c("HG002","HG005","HG01243","HG01109")),
    Chemistry = factor(Chemistry, levels = c("R10.4","R9.4.1")),
    Class = factor(Class, levels = c("A","B","U"))
  )

# Optional: nicely rounded version for the Supplementary Table
metrics_table <- metrics_df |>
  mutate(across(where(is.numeric), ~ round(.x, 6)))

# Export (choose one)
write.csv(metrics_table, "Supplementary_Table_ModelPerformance.csv", row.names = FALSE)

plot_df <- metrics_df |>
  select(Dataset, Chemistry, Class,
         Sensitivity, Specificity, Precision, Recall, F1, `Balanced Accuracy`) |>
  pivot_longer(
    cols = c(Sensitivity, Specificity, Precision, Recall, F1, `Balanced Accuracy`),
    names_to = "Metric",
    values_to = "Value"
  ) |>
  mutate(
    Metric = factor(Metric,
                    levels = c("F1","Balanced Accuracy","Precision","Recall","Sensitivity","Specificity"))
  )

p_perf <- ggplot(plot_df, aes(x = Dataset, y = Value, shape = Class, color = Chemistry)) +
  geom_point(size = 3, position = position_dodge(width = 0.45)) +
  facet_wrap(~Metric, ncol = 3, scales = "fixed") +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0.98, 1.0), breaks = seq(0.98, 1.0, by = 0.005)) +
  labs(
    title = "RFHap model performance across datasets",
    x = NULL,
    y = "Metric value",
    color = "Chemistry",
    shape = "Class"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 30, hjust = 1),
    strip.text = element_text(face = "bold"),
    legend.position = "bottom"
  )

p_perf
pdf("Model_metrics.pdf", width = 6, height = 4)
p_perf
dev.off()


f1_df <- metrics_df |>
  select(Dataset, Chemistry, Class, F1) |>
  mutate(
    Dataset = factor(Dataset, levels = c("HG002","HG005","HG01243","HG01109")),
    Chemistry = factor(Chemistry, levels = c("R10.4","R9.4.1")),
    Class = factor(Class, levels = c("A","B","U"))
  )

p_f1= ggplot(f1_df, aes(x = Dataset, y = F1, shape = Class, color = Chemistry)) +
  geom_point(size = 3, position = position_dodge(width = 0.45)) +
  #facet_wrap(~Metric, ncol = 3, scales = "fixed") +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0.98, 1.0), breaks = seq(0.98, 1.0, by = 0.005)) +
  labs(
    #title = "RFHap model performance across datasets",
    x = NULL,
    y = "F1",
    color = "Chemistry",
    shape = "Class"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    #panel.grid = element_blank(),
    strip.text = element_text(face = "plain", size = 13),
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 30, hjust = 1, vjust = 1, size = 11),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 11),
    legend.title = element_text(size = 11),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 10),
    legend.position = "bottom"
  )

p_f1

```


model feature importance 
```{r}
#datasets HG002, HG005, HG01109, and HG01243



#HG002
rf_model_hg002=readRDS("/mnt/beegfs/labs/DiGenomaLab/rfhap/processing/rfhap/results/HG002/HG002_dataset/rf/train/HG002_standar_ont_sup_PAW70337.fastq_fastkm_matrix_rf_model.rds")
#HG01109
rf_model_hg01109=readRDS("/mnt/beegfs/labs/DiGenomaLab/rfhap/processing/rfhap/new_datasets/HG01109/results/rf/train/HG01109_1_R941_Guppy_6.1.2.fq_fastkm_matrix_rf_model.rds")
#HG005
rf_model_hg005=readRDS("/mnt/beegfs/labs/DiGenomaLab/rfhap/processing/rfhap/hg005_v2/results/rf/train/HG005_standar_ont_sup_PAW88001.fastq_fastkm_matrix_rf_model.rds")
#HG01243
rf_model_hg01243=readRDS("/mnt/beegfs/labs/DiGenomaLab/rfhap/processing/rfhap/new_datasets/HG01243/results/rf/train/HG01243_1_R941_Guppy_6.1.2.fq_fastkm_matrix_rf_model.rds")




#we merge feature importance per dataset 
rf_importance=cbind(rf_model_hg002$importance,rf_model_hg005$importance,rf_model_hg01243$importance,rf_model_hg01109$importance)
colnames(rf_importance)=c("HG002","HG005","HG01243","HG01109")





library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(tibble)
library(ggplot2)
library(forcats)

# long format
imp_long <- as.data.frame(rf_importance) |>
  rownames_to_column("feature") |>
  pivot_longer(cols = -feature, names_to = "dataset", values_to = "gini") |>
  mutate(
    dataset = factor(dataset, levels = c("HG002","HG005","HG01243","HG01109")),
    chemistry = if_else(dataset %in% c("HG002","HG005"), "R10.4", "R9.4.1")
  ) |>
  group_by(dataset) |>
  mutate(gini_pct = 100 * gini / sum(gini)) |>
  ungroup()

top_n <- 15

# pick shared top features by mean % importance across datasets
feat_order <- imp_long |>
  group_by(feature) |>
  summarise(mean_pct = mean(gini_pct, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") |>
  arrange(desc(mean_pct)) |>
  slice_head(n = top_n) |>
  pull(feature)

heat_df <- imp_long |>
  filter(feature %in% feat_order) |>
  mutate(
    # put MOST important at TOP: make highest-importance features the LAST levels
    feature = factor(feature, levels = rev(feat_order))
  )

a=ggplot(heat_df, aes(x = dataset, y = feature, fill = gini_pct)) +
  geom_tile(color = "white", linewidth = 0.35) +
  geom_text(aes(label = sprintf("%.1f", gini_pct)),color = "white", size = 3.2) +
  facet_grid(. ~ chemistry, scales = "free_x", space = "free_x") +
  scale_fill_viridis_c(option = "magma", name = "% total\nGini") +
  labs(x = NULL, y = NULL) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    panel.grid = element_blank(),
    strip.text = element_text(face = "plain", size = 13),
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 30, hjust = 1, vjust = 1, size = 11),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 11),
    legend.title = element_text(size = 11),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 10),
    legend.position = "right",
    #plot.margin = margin(5, 8, 5, 5)
  )

# chem_sum from previous step (has R10.4, R9.4.1, delta, abs_delta)
chem_sum <- imp_long |>
  group_by(chemistry, feature) |>
  summarise(mean_pct = mean(gini_pct, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") |>
  pivot_wider(names_from = chemistry, values_from = mean_pct) |>
  mutate(
    delta = `R10.4` - `R9.4.1`,
    abs_delta = abs(delta),
    direction = if_else(delta >= 0, "Higher in R10.4", "Higher in R9.4.1")
  )

plot_df <- chem_sum |>
  slice_max(abs_delta, n = top_n, with_ties = FALSE) |>
  mutate(feature = fct_reorder(feature, delta))  # orders by signed delta

b=ggplot(plot_df, aes(x = delta, y = feature, fill = direction)) +
  geom_col(width = 0.8) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linewidth = 0.8) +
  geom_text(aes(label = sprintf("%+.2f", delta)),
            hjust = ifelse(plot_df$delta > 0, -0.15, 1.15),
            size = 3.6, show.legend = FALSE) +
  labs(
   # title = "Chemistry effect on RF feature importance",
   # subtitle = "Δ = mean(%Gini) in R10.4 minus R9.4.1 (top shifts)",
    #x = "Δ % Gini (R10.4 − R9.4.1)",
    x= NULL,
    y = NULL,
    fill = NULL
  ) +
  coord_cartesian(clip = "off") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    panel.grid.major.y = element_blank(),
    legend.position = "bottom",
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
    #plot.margin = margin(10, 60, 10, 10)
  )

a+b

```


###Prediction
```{r}
library(data.table)
prob = read.table("/mnt/beegfs/labs/DiGenomaLab/rfhap/processing/rfhap/results/HG002/HG002_dataset/rf/prediction/HG002_standar_ont_sup_PAW71238.fastq_fastkm_matrix.9_rf_prediction.txt", sep =" ", header =TRUE)

library(ggplot2)
library(scales)

fill_colors <- c(
  "A" = alpha("#66C2A5", 0.18),
  "B" = alpha("steelblue2", 0.18),
  "U" = alpha("lightsalmon", 0.18)
)

border_colors <- c(
  "A" = "#2CA58D",
  "B" = "#2B6CB0",
  "U" = "#F28E6B"
)

# optional: if there are MANY points, downsample to keep the figure readable
set.seed(1)
prob_plot_df <- prob
# prob_plot_df <- prob |> dplyr::slice_sample(n = min(30000, nrow(prob)))  # uncomment if needed

prob_plot <- ggplot(
  prob_plot_df,
  aes(
    x = Predicted.prob.A,
    y = Predicted.prob.B,
    fill  = Predicted.Class,
    color = Predicted.Class
  )
) +
  # Simplex boundary A + B <= 1  (triangle)
  geom_abline(intercept = 1, slope = -1, linewidth = 0.8, linetype = "22", color = "grey35") +
  annotate("segment", x = 0, xend = 1, y = 0, yend = 0, linewidth = 0.6, color = "grey35") +
  annotate("segment", x = 0, xend = 0, y = 0, yend = 1, linewidth = 0.6, color = "grey35") +

  # Optional class boundaries if your rule is argmax(A,B,U) with U = 1-A-B
  # A = B   (split A vs B)
  #geom_abline(intercept = 0, slope = 1, linewidth = 0.6, linetype = "33", color = "grey55") +
  # A = U -> A = (1-A-B) -> 2A + B = 1 -> B = 1 - 2A
  #geom_abline(intercept = 1, slope = -2, linewidth = 0.6, linetype = "33", color = "grey55") +
  # B = U -> A + 2B = 1 -> B = (1-A)/2
  #geom_abline(intercept = 0.5, slope = -0.5, linewidth = 0.6, linetype = "33", color = "grey55") +

  geom_point(
    shape  = 21,
    size   = 1.6,      # smaller!
    stroke = 0.25,     # thinner border
    alpha  = 0.55      # extra transparency
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = fill_colors, drop = FALSE) +
  scale_color_manual(values = border_colors, drop = FALSE) +
  coord_fixed(xlim = c(0,1), ylim = c(0,1), expand = FALSE) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 1, 0.1)) +
  scale_y_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 1, 0.1)) +
  labs(
    x = "P(class A)",
    y = "P(class B)",
    fill  = "Predicted class",
    color = "Predicted class"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  theme(
    axis.text = element_text(color = "black"),
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 0),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    legend.position = "bottom",
    legend.box = "horizontal"
  )

prob_plot



```
##Venn
```{r}
merged_all = readRDS("/mnt/beegfs/labs/DiGenomaLab/rfhap/analysis/classification_comparison/random_forest/train-v03/intersection_methods.rds")
df = merged_all
head(df)
```

### figure
```{r}

library(ggplot2)
library(ggforce)

# vectores de etiquetas (ya en tu entorno)
A <- as.character(df$trio_binning)
B <- as.character(df$hifiasm)
C <- as.character(df$rfhap)

n <- length(A)

# regiones mutuamente excluyentes CORRECTAS
ABC      <- sum(A == B & B == C)                          # los tres iguales
AB_only  <- sum(A == B & A != C)                          # A y B iguales, C distinto
AC_only  <- sum(A == C & A != B)                          # A y C iguales, B distinto
BC_only  <- sum(B == C & B != A)                          # B y C iguales, A distinto
all_diff <- sum(A != B & A != C & B != C)                 # las tres diferentes

# sanity check
sum_regions <- ABC + AB_only + AC_only + BC_only + all_diff
stopifnot(sum_regions == n)   # esto debe pasar

res <- data.frame(
  categoria = c("los_tres_coinciden", "trio_binning_hifiasm", "trio_binning_no_norm_trainv03",
                "hifiasm_no_norm_trainv03", "ninguna_coincide"),
  count = c(ABC, AB_only, AC_only, BC_only, all_diff),
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)
res$percent <- 100 * res$count / n
res$percent <- round(res$percent, 2)

print(res)
cat("Suma porcentajes:", sum(res$percent), "\n")  # ~100 (puede haber redondeo)

labels_pos <- data.frame(
  categoria = c(
    "trio_binning_hifiasm",         # AB_only
    "trio_binning_no_norm_trainv03",# AC_only
    "hifiasm_no_norm_trainv03",     # BC_only
    "los_tres_coinciden",           # ABC
    "ninguna_coincide"              # all_diff
  ),
  x = c(
    0.60,   # AB_only
    0.10,   # AC_only (más al centro y arriba)
    1.2,   # BC_only (más al centro y arriba)
    0.60,   # ABC
    1.90    # all_diff
  ),
  y = c(
    -0.25,  # AB_only
    0.85,   # AC_only → centrado mejor
    0.85,   # BC_only → centrado mejor
    0.40,   # ABC
    1.60    # all_diff
  ),
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)

label_df <- merge(labels_pos, res, by = "categoria", all.x = TRUE)
label_df$label <- paste0(label_df$count, "\n", label_df$percent, "%")

# -------------------------------------------------------------
# CÍRCULOS
# -------------------------------------------------------------
circles <- data.frame(
  name = c("trio_binning", "hifiasm", "rfhap"),
  x0 = c(0.0, 1.2, 0.6),
  y0 = c(0.0, 0.0, 1.05),
  r  = c(1.05, 1.05, 1.05)
)

# -------------------------------------------------------------
# TÍTULOS DE LOS CÍRCULOS 
# -------------------------------------------------------------
circle_labels <- transform(
  circles,
  lab_x = x0,
  lab_y = ifelse(name == "rfhap", y0 + r + 0.15, y0 - r - 0.15)
)


palette_paper <- c(
  "trio_binning" = "#66C2A5",  # azul medio
  "hifiasm"      = "steelblue2",  # azul claro–medio
  "rfhap"        = "lightsalmon"   # naranjo medio
)

# -------------------------------------------------------------
# GRÁFICO FINAL
# -------------------------------------------------------------
p <- ggplot() +
  geom_circle(data = circles,
              aes(x0 = x0, y0 = y0, r = r, fill = name),
              color = "black", alpha = 0.35, size = 0.4) +

  # etiquetas de intersecciones
  geom_text(data = label_df,
            aes(x = x, y = y, label = label),
            size = 7, lineheight = 0.9) +

  # títulos de los círculos
  geom_text(data = circle_labels,
            aes(x = lab_x, y = lab_y, label = name),
            size = 9) +

  coord_fixed(xlim = c(-1.2, 2.3), ylim = c(-1.2, 2.2)) +
  theme_void() +
  theme(text=element_text(size=27), plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5,size = 30, face = "bold"), legend.position = "none")+
  scale_fill_manual(values = palette_paper)
 # scale_fill_manual(values = c(
  #  "trio_binning" = "#F8766D",
   # "hifiasm"      = "#00BA38",
    #"rfhap"        = "#619CFF"
  #))

#print(p)
p
```



## Upset plot

```{r upset}
library(UpSetR)
library(grid)

A <- as.character(df$trio_binning)
B <- as.character(df$hifiasm)
C <- as.character(df$rfhap)


sets_list <- list(
  `Trio binning = Hifiasm` = which(A == B),
  `Trio binning = RFHap`   = which(A == C),
  `Hifiasm = RFHap`        = which(B == C),
  `All three agree`        = which(A == B & B == C),
  `All different`          = which(A != B & A != C & B != C))

# set2_col <- "steelblue"
# 
# plot_importance <- varimplot %>%
#   filter(MeanDecreaseGini > 500) %>%
#   mutate(feature = fct_reorder(feature, MeanDecreaseGini)) %>%
#   ggplot(aes(
#     x = MeanDecreaseGini,
#     y = feature,
#     size = MeanDecreaseGini        
#   )) +
#     geom_point(
#     shape  = 21,              
#     fill   = "steelblue",     
#     color  = "steelblue4",    
#     stroke = 1.2,            
#     alpha  = 0.6
#   ) +
#   scale_size_continuous(
#     range = c(3, 10),              
#     guide = "none"                 
#   ) +
#   theme_minimal() +
#   labs(
#     x = "MeanDecreaseGini",
#     y = "Feature"
#   ) +
#   theme(
#     text = element_text(size = 27),
#     axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1, color = "black"), axis.text.y = element_text(color = "black")
#   )
# 
# png("importance.png", width = 1000 , height = 800)
# plot_importance
# dev.off()
# plot_importance
```

###Prediction
```{r}
# library(data.table)
# prob = read.table("/mnt/beegfs/labs/DiGenomaLab/rfhap/processing/rfhap/results/HG002/HG002_dataset/rf/prediction/HG002_standar_ont_sup_PAW71238.fastq_fastkm_matrix.2_rf_prediction.txt", sep =" ", header =TRUE)

#table(prob$Predicted.Class)

```

```{r}
# fill_colors <- c(
#   "A" = alpha("#66C2A5", 0.25),    
#   "B" = alpha("steelblue2", 0.25),
#   "U" = alpha("lightsalmon", 0.25)
# >>>>>>> refs/remotes/origin/main
# )

upset_input <- fromList(sets_list)

# nicer names + stable order (top-to-bottom)
set_order <- c(
  "Trio binning = Hifiasm",
  "Trio binning = RFHap",
  "Hifiasm = RFHap",
  "All three agree",
  "All different"
)

# format bar labels with commas
lab_commas <- function(x) format(x, big.mark = ",", scientific = FALSE)



  upset(
  upset_input,
  sets = set_order,
  keep.order = TRUE,

  # show the most relevant intersections first (largest)
  order.by = "freq",
  nintersects = 10,

  # layout
  mb.ratio = c(0.65, 0.35),

  # labels
  mainbar.y.label = "intersection size",
  sets.x.label    = "set size",
  text.scale      = c(1.7, 1.4, 1.2, 1.2, 1.4, 1.2),

  # colors
  main.bar.color = "#2B6CB0",   # blue
  sets.bar.color = "#2F855A",   # green
  matrix.color   = "#444444",   # dark grey

  # show counts on top of intersection bars
  show.numbers = TRUE,
  #number.angles = 0

  # NOTE: UpSetR can't directly format show.numbers labels,
  # but the larger font + color helps readability.
)

# 2) record it
d_rec <- recordPlot()

# 3) convert the recorded plot into a grob (THIS is what patchwork needs)
d_grob <- grid.grabExpr(replayPlot(d_rec))

# 4) wrap grob for patchwork
d <- wrap_elements(full = d_grob)




```

```{r to}
library(UpSetR)

# Avoid scientific notation in bar labels
options(scipen = 999)

make_upset_for_class <- function(df, cls,
                                 highlight = "#2B6CB0",
                                 neutral_main = "grey35",
                                 sets_bar = "grey70",
                                 matrix_col = "grey30") {

  # Build sets: reads called == cls by each method
  sets_list <- list(
    "Trio binning" = which(df$trio_binning == cls),
    "Hifiasm"      = which(df$hifiasm == cls),
    "RFHap"        = which(df$rfhap == cls)
  )

  input <- fromList(sets_list)

  upset(
    input,
    sets = c("Trio binning", "Hifiasm", "RFHap"),
    keep.order = TRUE,

    # show the largest overlaps first
    order.by = c("freq", "degree"),
    nintersects = 7,

    # better sizing / readability
    mb.ratio = c(0.65, 0.35),
    point.size = 3.2,
    line.size = 1.1,
    text.scale = c(1.6, 1.3, 1.2, 1.2, 1.3, 1.1),

    # axis labels (explicit about the class)
    mainbar.y.label = paste0("Haplotype '", cls, "' (intersection size)"),
    sets.x.label    = paste0("Haplotype '", cls, "' (set size)"),

    # base colors
    main.bar.color = neutral_main,
    sets.bar.color = sets_bar,
    matrix.color   = matrix_col,

    # highlight the key message: all three methods agree on this class
    queries = list(
      list(
        query = intersects,
        params = list("Trio binning", "Hifiasm", "RFHap"),
        color = highlight,
        active = TRUE
      )
    ),

    show.numbers = TRUE,
    #number.angles = 0
  )
}

# --- Use consistent colors per class (paper-friendly) ---
# A = green, B = blue, U = orange
make_upset_for_class(merged_all, "A", highlight = "#2F855A")  # green
make_upset_for_class(merged_all, "B", highlight = "#2B6CB0")  # blue
make_upset_for_class(merged_all, "U", highlight = "#F28E2B")  # orange

```

## Merge plots 

```{r}
library(patchwork)

# 3) Combine everything (a, b, prob_plot are ggplot objects)
p2 <- ((p_f1|a) / (b | d)) +
  plot_annotation(
    tag_levels = "A",
    theme = theme(
      plot.tag = element_text(size = 16, face = "bold")
    )
  )
p2
pdf("Figure1.pdf", width = 10, height = 10)
p2
dev.off()
```
